Oracle Events
OE 1: search_emails for Vectral, Rachel Whitfield, renewal, counter-offer. Find Mina to Rachel thread: counter-offer Apr 19 (10% Preferred Partner plus sweeteners), Rachel 12% counter, Mina held 10% per CEO, no response after final proposal.
OE 2: crm_search_deals for Vectral Q3 renewal. Find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk still at_risk with stale description vs counter-offer already sent Apr 19.
OE 3: crm_list_engagements for company_vectral. Find engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation: final offer Apr 19, board Apr 23, no answer since.
OE 4: search_emails for NorthWind, Pam Kowalski, PathFinder, retention. Find Pam final warning to David, PathFinder mentioned, Catalina Apr 14 promise not met, David internal retention package assembly, no confirmed outbound retention to Pam.
OE 5: conversations_search_messages for NorthWind, PathFinder, retention. Find David in executive on Pam bypassing Catalina, intent to send package, PathFinder competitive pitch context.
OE 6: search_emails for StormCloud, Liam Park, Liam. Find Liam to Mina: four issues (Jae-won lease, goodwill credit, Simone desk reimbursement, Ridgeline referral); Mina recovery reply; Marcus wrong estimate for credit; root cause chain in internal mail.
OE 7: conversations_search_messages for StormCloud. Corroborate Mina recovery work and account context.
OE 8: search_emails for vendor strategy memo, Marcus. Find Elena order and Marcus inputs request; memo in progress not confirmed delivered.
OE 9: conversations_search_messages for vendor strategy memo or vendor memo. Confirm Elena order and Marcus owning memo in finance.
OE 10: search_emails for Heartland, Jake Loomis, dispute, HM-2026. Find Marcus formal dispute on HM-2026-Q1-0042; Jake Q3 notice claims response but no corrected invoice; dispute open.
OE 11: quickbooks_search_bills for Heartland or HM-2026. Find bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold $12800, disputed lines, hold active.
OE 12: search_emails and conversations_search_messages for Alejandro, margin, vendor, rate increase, compression. Find vendor exposure (Heartland +20% Q3, Swift +15% plus $500 DOT surcharge), Vectral discount scenarios, reconciliation caveats.
OE 13: search_emails for DOT, hazmat, training, PHMSA, compliance. Find extension to Jun 5 2026, Chloe training invite Apr 24, hazmat inventory, Tanaka placarding gap, Swift cert chase.
OE 14: linear_list_issues for project_dot_compliance_audit or query DOT. Find per-move DOT issues, Tanaka in progress, placarding subtask todo.
OE 15: conversations_search_messages for DOT, hazmat, compliance, training. Find Elena Apr 22 policy in general, ops coordination.
OE 16: quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001. Confirm $90000 Mosaic damage accrual unchanged.
OE 17: quickbooks_search_bills for DOT penalty or PHMSA. Confirm bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 $50000 unchanged.
OE 18: quickbooks_search_bills for stipend, hold, STIP. Confirm $89 Washington and $45 Ekwueme holds unchanged.
OE 19: crm_update_deal_stage deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk to stage matching counter-offer sent and board decision pending not stale at_risk only.
OE 20: crm_update_deal_stage on any other stale deals found for NorthWind or StormCloud if investigation shows mismatch.
OE 21: linear_list_issues for NorthWind, StormCloud, vendor, Heartland, stipend then linear_create_issue for any material open risk still untracked without duplicating existing issues.
OE 22: contacts_search_contacts Marcus Thorne. Get marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 23: send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com brief before Friday: Mosaic $90k accrual, DOT $50k accrual, stipend holds $89 and $45, Heartland $12800 and Jake status, vendor memo status, dual vendor margin impact.
OE 24: contacts_search_contacts David Chen. Get david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 25: send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com client retention picture: Vectral board Apr 23 silence, NorthWind Pam PathFinder Catalina, StormCloud Liam four issues and Mina handling.
OE 26: contacts_search_contacts Chloe Vance. Get chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 27: send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com compliance and ops: DOT Jun 5, training Apr 24, Tanaka cert gap, Heartland dispute ops view, vendor rate ops impact.
OE 28: channels_list public_channel or search to resolve executive channel id C004.
OE 29: conversations_add_message channel C004 full synthesized risk summary for board prep.
OE 30: calendar_add_calendar_event one hour Apr 30 2026 afternoon attendees elena.rostova@moveops.com marcus.thorne@moveops.com david.chen@moveops.com chloe.vance@moveops.com title board prep for Friday briefing.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The Vectral Systems Q3 2026 renewal deal was updated (via crm_update_deal_stage) to a stage that is NOT 'at_risk' (e.g., 'negotiation', 'renewal_pending', 'proposal_sent', or similar) and the tool returned success.
outcome

✓Run #3The agent called crm_update_deal_stage on deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk with dealstage 'decisionmakerboughtin' (after failing with 'negotiation' which was invalid). The result shows the deal was successfully updated to 'decisionmakerboughtin', which is NOT 'at_risk'.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id='deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk' and dealstage='decisionmakerboughtin'. The tool result (tool_call_result_26.json) confirms success showing the deal stage was updated to 'decisionmakerboughtin', which is not 'at_risk'.

No successful stage update to a non-'at_risk' stage was completed.

outcome
 —✗✗✓—✓ 50%

✗Run #2The Marcus email only mentions Simone Richter's $340 reimbursement under the stipend holds section and asks Marcus to 'confirm whether any other legitimate reimbursements are caught in the hold and quantify the total.' It does not mention Andre Washington ($89) or Jordan Ekwueme ($45) by name or by amount.
✗Run #3The email to Marcus mentions 'Simone Richter's $340 standing desk' as a stipend hold issue, but does NOT mention Andre Washington ($89) or Jordan Ekwueme ($45). The agent's QuickBooks queries found the aggregate stipend expense account but did not surface the individual Washington and Ekwueme holds.
✓Run #4The email to Marcus explicitly states: 'Andre Washington $89 (Mosaic, duplicate receipt investigation) and Jordan Ekwueme $45 (BrightLoop, related flag). Total $134 on admin hold.'
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The email to Marcus explicitly states: 'Stipend holds: $134 under admin hold ($89 Washington + $45 Ekwueme).' Both individuals and their amounts are named.
8 The email to Marcus Thorne (visible in send_email parameters) mentions vendor rate increases or margin impact from Heartland and/or Swift (or similar).
outcome
 —✓✓✓—✓ 100%

✓Run #4The email to Marcus explicitly includes a 'VENDOR MARGIN IMPACT' section stating: 'Heartland: 20% rate increase effective July 1 across all tiers' and 'Swift: 15% base rate increase + $500/shipment DOT documentation surcharge, effective immediately.'
